books.title: Hadji Murád
books.author: Leo Tolstoy
books.summary: The legendary warrior Hadji Murád struggles to free his family against the backdrop of Russian imperialism in Chechnya and Dagestan.

books.title: Journals
books.author: Alexander Mackenzie
books.summary: Explorer Alexander Mackenzie details his journeys to both the Arctic and Pacific Oceans in his personal journals.

books.title: Confessions of an English Opium-Eater
books.author: Thomas De Quincey
books.summary: A portrait of the life of a drug addict in Georgian-era London.

books.title: Personal Recollections of Joan of Arc
books.author: Mark Twain
books.summary: A fictional biography of Joan of Arc from her early teenage years to her martyrdom.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Beatrix Potter
books.summary: A collection of Beatrix Potter’s short stories for children, ordered by date of publication.

books.title: Jurgen
books.author: James Branch Cabell
books.summary: Jurgen, a pawnbroker and “monstrous clever fellow,” is sent on a witty and parodic magical journey in search of true love.

books.title: Three Sisters
books.author: Anton Chekhov
books.summary: Three sisters long to escape their provincial town and return to Moscow.

books.title: The Phoenix and the Carpet
books.author: E. Nesbit
books.summary: Five children once again encounter an ancient, magical creature and go on adventures around the world with its magic carpet.

books.title: On the Eve
books.author: Ivan Turgenev
books.summary: A young Russian woman falls in love with a Bulgarian patriot on the eve of the Crimean War.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Arthur Machen
books.summary: A collection of nine short fiction works by influential supernatural and horror author Arthur Machen.

books.title: The Education of Henry Adams
books.author: Henry Adams
books.summary: A prominent and caustic historian reflects on the reasons that “the more he was educated, the less he understood,” and what he believes the future holds for American society.

books.title: The Experiences of Loveday Brooke, Lady Detective
books.author: Catherine Louisa Pirkis
books.summary: A female private investigator solves crimes in late-Victorian England.

books.title: Now It Can Be Told
books.author: Philip Gibbs
books.summary: A British war correspondent recalls his experiences during the Great War.

books.title: Some Thoughts Concerning Education
books.author: John Locke
books.summary: John Locke outlines his philosophy of childhood education.

books.title: Magnolia Leaves
books.author: Mary Weston Fordham
books.summary: A collection of poems by Mary Weston Fordham.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Ring Lardner
books.summary: A collection of short stories by Ring Lardner.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Mack Reynolds
books.summary: A collection of science fiction short stories by Mack Reynolds ordered by date of publication.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: H. P. Lovecraft
books.summary: The complete U.S. public domain short fiction of horror legend H. P. Lovecraft.

books.title: Barlaam and Ioasaph
books.author: Anonymous
books.summary: A monk converts a cloistered young prince to Christianity.

books.title: Letters Written During a Short Residence in Sweden, Norway, and Denmark
books.author: Mary Wollstonecraft
books.summary: Mary Wollstonecraft’s account of her travels through Scandinavia.
